text.title: Trend Research Pipeline
text.summary: Two-pass pipeline for trend research: broad signal collection, then scored triage using recency, evidence, and applicability dimensions.

section.title: Two-Pass Pipeline
section.p: Pass 1 (Opus-led): strategic scan ingests the four trend axes, emits a structured JSON spec per signal with fields: trend, signal, source_url, confidence (0-1), impact (H/M/L). Pass 2 (Sonnet-led, parallel): one subagent per trend axis fetches WebSearch + WebFetch evidence and writes one section. Final reviewer subagent merges sections, deduplicates, and flags unsourced claims.

rule.statement: Human must approve the Pass 1 JSON spec (the 4-6 trend axes and their framing) before Pass 2 sweeps begin.
rule.rationale: Pass 2 costs significant WebSearch quota. If the axes are wrong, all that work is wasted. The checkpoint is cheap; the correction is not.

section.title: Signal Scoring
section.p: Score each signal on three dimensions, max 9 points total. Drop any signal scoring below 5.

list.item: Recency: 3 if published within 30 days, 2 if 31-90 days, 1 if 91-180 days, 0 if older than 180 days
Evidence: 3 if primary source (vendor changelog, primary research), 2 if 3+ independent citations, 1 if secondary aggregator
Applicability: 0-3 assigned by the human reviewer based on fit to this specific product context

section.reference: Python script implementing recency/evidence scoring and threshold filtering

section.title: Output Structure

rule.statement: Date-stamp every claim at the bullet level, not just the document. Future agents diff bullet-dates to detect staleness.
rule.rationale: Document-level dates hide which specific claims aged out. Bullet-level dates let the next quarterly run surgically replace stale items rather than regenerating the entire brief.

rule.statement: Include a "kill list" section: for every trend you adopt, name one current practice you stop.
rule.rationale: Trend reports without subtractions are theatre. A kill list forces the team to make a real trade-off and signals which prior commitments are being abandoned.

section.p: Persist the raw signals as .aidocs/research/raw/signals.jsonl. The diff between this quarter and the previous file is more useful than the polished narrative.

list.item: faion-research-agent (mode: market or ideas) — orchestrator for trend signal collection
faion-sdd-executor-agent — converts accepted trend implications into backlog/ features with acceptance criteria

rule.statement: Route any feature spawned by trend research through faion-sdd-executor-agent with explicit acceptance criteria. "Act on trend X" is not a feature.
rule.rationale: Trend-to-feature translation without acceptance criteria produces vague tickets that no one can implement or verify.
